comments.body: HANDOFF (2026-07-08): fsck --repair tooling LANDED on main (Gerrit 407) + resurrection-repair edge fix (Gerrit 410). Ticket still in_progress — completion verifier requires 5 more items before close: (1) test the push-failure ABORT path in _repair_run (fsck.py ~598-602); (2) implement + test the 'verify no in-flight pass' check for the reconciler pause (currently only gh workflow disable/enable in finally, no in-flight check); (3) crash-before-marker safety test (.a3-repaired under <gitdir>/a3-repaired/); (4) synthetic MISSING_CREATE fixture test (fsck --repair surfaces, does NOT auto-write); (5) THE LIVE REMEDIATION RUN: rebar fsck --repair on the shared store (~2422 SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT + 11 ORPHAN + 1 MISSING_CREATE), record before/after counts in an ops note. Existing tests: test_fsck_snapshot_checks.py (dispositions/retire/dry-run/live-retire), test_concurrency_regression.py (a3 pretag+rollback, resurrection). Then close (require_completion_verification_for_close is on).

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body:
## A3 LIVE remediation — ops note (2026-07-08)

**Store:** shared `tickets` branch (origin/tickets). **Tool:** `rebar fsck --repair` (this worktree's local build, incl. the reconciler in-flight guard).

### Before → After (fsck fault counts)
| Fault class | Before | After | Disposition |
|---|---|---|---|
| SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT | 2422 | **0** | auto-retired (rename folded source → `.retired`); incl. 4 folded-OLDER-snapshot cases fixed this pass |
| ORPHAN_EVENT | 13 | 13 | HUMAN-TRIAGE (all order-sensitive EDIT); left surfaced, NOT auto-written |
| MISSING_CREATE | 1 | 1 | HUMAN-TRIAGE; surfaced, never auto-written |

Result pushed to origin/tickets @ `47d917f6`. Auto-recoverable classes → **zero**.

### Reconciler safety
- Disabled `reconcile-bridge.yml` for the window; **re-enabled** after (now `active`).
- The in-flight guard correctly ABORTED an initial attempt while a live pass held `refs/reconciler/lock` (the pass was heartbeating). Waited for the pass to finish, then ran cleanly.
- Pre-tag `pre-a3-remediation` written before any write (rollback point).

### Tooling gap found + fixed
`_repair_plan` excluded every `*-SNAPSHOT.json` from retire candidates, so a re-compaction that folds an OLDER snapshot into a newer one's `source_event_uuids` left the still-present older snapshot as an unrepaired SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT (4 tickets: 72d5, b043, ce94, fc9e). Fixed: older snapshots are now retire candidates (never the horizon `latest_snap`), with a unit test.

### Human-triage disposition (empirically validated — the plan's routing is CORRECT)
The 13 ORPHAN_EVENTs are all pre-snapshot **EDIT** events (assignee edits) absent from `source_event_uuids`. I tested the deterministic full-log rebuild (`--repair-snapshots`) on them and **it CORRUPTED state**: 11 of 13 tickets flipped `closed → in_progress` (the rebuild reorders STATUS events). **Reverted before any push** — no bad data reached origin. This confirms EDIT orphans must stay HUMAN-TRIAGE (auto-rebuild is unsafe), exactly as the A3 orphan-disposition constant routes them. Follow-up ticket filed for manual reconciliation.

compiled_state.description:
# Live-store remediation: drive fsck to zero (safe, reversible, batched, committed)

## Why
`rebar fsck` on the live store = 2422 SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT + 11 ORPHAN_EVENT + 1 MISSING_CREATE. Fix #1 stopped
new corruption; this cleans the backlog. DESTRUCTIVE on a live, git-backed shared store — safety is first-class.
Consumes NEW sibling deliverables: A1 (tricolour-head-ratfish) ADDS the `.retired` rename + `RETIRED_SUFFIX` +
retire helper; A2 (glandulous-potent-waxwing) ADDS the `include_retired` rebuild path. depends_on A1+A2.

## Repair per fault class
- SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT (2422): a folded source file STILL PRESENT (listed in `source_event_uuids`, never deleted).
  Repair = call A1's retire helper to rename each still-present listed source to `.retired`. NOT a rebuild.
- ORPHAN_EVENT (11): a pre-snapshot event absent from `source_event_uuids`. Routed BY EVENT TYPE (see policy):
  AUTO-RECOVER types -> A2's `reduce_ticket(..., include_retired=True)` rebuild; HUMAN-TRIAGE types -> surfaced,
  NOT auto-rebuilt (the rebuild would pick an order that may be wrong).
- MISSING_CREATE (1): `fsck --repair` logs it, no automatic write; human triage.

## Orphan disposition (a CODE constant, not just prose)
Define two sets in code (enumerating ALL KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES so none is unclassified):
- AUTO-RECOVER (additive/commutative): COMMENT, LINK, UNLINK, TAG_DELTA, COMMITS, BRIDGE_ALERT, REVERT.
- HUMAN-TRIAGE (order-sensitive): STATUS, EDIT, FILE_IMPACT, VERIFY_COMMANDS, SIGNATURE, WORKFLOW_RUN,
  WORKFLOW_STEP, ARCHIVED. Decisions recorded in the PR / a linked ops doc.

## Safety
- GIT-AWARE writes: the tracker is a git worktree. Each batch commits+pushes:
  `git -C <tracker> add -A && git -C <tracker> commit -m 'a3-remediation: batch N/M' && git -C <tracker> push`.
  If a push FAILS (conflict/network/reject), ABORT the run and surface the error — do NOT continue; the pre-tag
  enables rollback. BEFORE any write: `git -C <tracker> tag pre-a3-remediation <oid>`; rollback = `git reset --hard
  pre-a3-remediation && git push --force-with-lease`.
- Dry-run FIRST: `fsck --repair --dry-run` prints per-ticket before/after diff + dispositions, writes NOTHING
  (a test asserts zero file writes + zero commits); the live run is gated on human review.
- Reconciler pause: disable the `reconcile-bridge.yml` GHA schedule for the window via the GitHub API/CLI
  (`gh workflow disable reconcile-bridge.yml`), verify no pass is mid-flight, and RE-ENABLE in a `finally`/failsafe
  (auto re-enable after N minutes or on repair-process exit) so a crash never leaves it disabled. (The pass-lock
  `refs/reconciler/lock` is a leased CAS lock and would expire, so it is NOT the pause mechanism.) Manual writers
  hold off (documented); the batched+committed+pre-tagged design makes a stray write recoverable.
- Idempotent/resumable: fsck itself is the AUTHORITATIVE resumability check (re-run repairs only tickets fsck still
  flags). A per-ticket `.a3-repaired` marker is a LOCAL, gitignored optimization only. Per-snapshot rewrite uses the
  EXISTING `rebar._store.fsutil.atomic_write` (not hand-rolled). The SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT rename + rebuild hold the
  store write-lock for the per-ticket critical section (no TOCTOU with a concurrent writer). Per-ticket failure
  logs+skips (no batch abort). Batched `--limit=200`, wait >=30s between.

## Scope
`_commands/fsck.py` ONLY (`--repair`/`--dry-run`/`--limit`, per-batch git commit+push, disposition constants,
per-snapshot `fsutil.atomic_write`, local marker, write-lock critical section; update the `fsck --help` golden files
`tests/golden/cli_help/fsck__help.out` + the `-h` variant). This ticket does NOT edit `compact.py` (A1 owns all
compact.py changes) — it CALLS A1's retire helper and A2's `include_retired` rebuild. Gated on RC1 fix #1.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `fsck --repair --dry-run` prints per-ticket before/after + dispositions; a test asserts ZERO file writes and ZERO commits in dry-run.
- [ ] Live repair commits+pushes each batch; a push failure ABORTS + surfaces the error; pre-tag created; rollback rehearsed on a fixture (post-rollback store == pre-tag state).
- [ ] Reconciler paused by `gh workflow disable` (verified no in-flight pass), re-enabled in a failsafe `finally`.
- [ ] Orphan disposition is a code constant covering ALL KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES; AUTO-RECOVER rebuilt, HUMAN-TRIAGE surfaced (not auto-rebuilt); tested.
- [ ] Resumability via fsck re-check; `.a3-repaired` marker local/gitignored; per-snapshot rewrite uses `fsutil.atomic_write`; write-lock held per ticket; crash-before-marker is safe (test).
- [ ] Synthetic-fixture unit tests: SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT / ORPHAN_EVENT (both dispositions) / MISSING_CREATE (incl. `.retired`) repair correctly.
- [ ] Live store reaches 0 UNRESOLVED faults (triaged+documented orphans count as resolved); before/after counts recorded.

file_impact.reason: re-fold from full ordered log incl .retired

compiled_state.title: Live-store corruption remediation: drive fsck to zero
